rulings.Case Title: BRASIL SF, LLC, DBA BOSSA NOVA VS. AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidence And/Or Terminating Sanctions [C.C.P. §§ 2023.030(B)-(D)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC'S Motion For Issue, Evidence And/Or Terminating Sanctions [C.C.P. §§ 2023.030(B)-(D)]. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for issue, evidence and/or terminating sanctions is conditionally denied. The plaintiff has represented on or before the hearing date all discovery referenced in this court's Order dated March 16, 2014 will have been served prior to the hearing today. If all discovery responses referenced in said order have not been served, a hearing is required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ORVILLE MEAUX VS. PAUL J. STEINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Paul J. Steiners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 2, Defendant Paul J. Steiner's Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel non-party attorney to respond to a deposition subpoena for production of its former client's records is denied. The documents sought by the defendant attorney are protected by the attorney-client privilege which can only be waived by the plaintiff. The filing of the lawsuit by the plaintiff against a successor attorney does not waive the attorney client privilege as to the non-sued (in this action) previous attorney. See: Miller v. Superior Court, 111 Cal. App. 3d 390. The case cited by the defendant, Hecht, Solberg, Robinson v. Superior Court, 137 Cal. App. 4th, is not relevant to the issues in this motion as it applies to a third-party attorney's resistance to production of its own financial records and not refusal to produce a former client's privileged litigation records. A hearing is required to determine if sanctions should be awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS WONG VS. ALAN ABRUZZO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 8, Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Inspection. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel inspection of real property owned by the defendant which is not the subject of this lawsuit is denied. The sole reason for seeking the real property inspection is not primarily to "measure, survey, photograph, test or sample the land or other property, or any designated object or operation on it". It is to view plaintiff's personal possessions which may be located within the property for evidence of defendant's present residence. Said reason is not sufficient good cause for a real property inspection. The inspection is also not likely to lead to the discovery of relevant evidence which concerns a different property because defendant's residence on June in 2015 is not probative of events occurring in 2012 and 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICARDO FRIAS VS. LAUNDRY LOCKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer And Consolidate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF RICARDO FRIAS' Motion To Transfer And Consolidate The court grants this motion under CCP 403 and CRC 3.500(c). Plaintiff's moving declaration complies with CRC 3.500(c). Par. 6 of counsel's declaration states that the cases are not complex. There are common issues of law and fact regarding causation and damages. Consolidation is necessary to prevent inconsistent rulings. Transfer and consolidation imposes a burden on Mr. Payne, but the prejudice to plaintiff without consolidation is more burdensome. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: MARC PEARL VS. VICKEY HOLMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Vickey Holmes, Amanda Holmes, Kaleidoscope Media Group Inc, Ultimate Business Success Llc, Vickey Holmes And Amanda Holmes As Co-Trustees Of Business Breakthroughs International'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas To Snell Wilmer And Joshua Schneiderman (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 3, Defendant Vickey Holmes, Amanda Holmes, Kaleidoscope Media Group Inc, Ultimate Business Success LLC, Vickey Holmes And Amanda Holmes As Co-Trustees Of Business Breakthroughs International's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas To Snell Wilmer And Joshua Schneiderman (added for posting of the tentative ruling) (part 2 of 2). No later than one week prior to the date set for the depositions, the parties shall obtain Discovery Referee rulings as to discoverability and claims of privilege as to the documents referenced in the subpoena; (2) The parties shall forthwith meet and confer with the Robbins Magnet entity regarding contractual confidentiality provided for in the Magnet, LLC/CHI/BBI settlement agreement. An attempt shall be made to reach agreement permitting confidential discovery of the "factual circumstances surrounding [the settlement]". See: Hinshaw, Winkler, Draa, Marsh v. Superior Court (1996) 51 Cal. App. 4th 233. If agreement is not reached the plaintiff shall file a motion before this Court for formal adjudication of said issue. The defendant shall also provide a privilege log as to documents withheld from deposition discovery pursuant to the mediation privilege, which may be the subject of a motion if in dispute. The depositions may not be scheduled until all discoverability issues pertaining to the Robbins Magnet settlement are resolved by agreement or adjudication. A further hearing on this motion is set for June 25, 2015 at 9:00 am in Department 302 to report the status of the depositions herewith permitted to be held. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARC PEARL VS. VICKEY HOLMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas To Snell Wilmer And Joshua Schneiderman
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT VICKEY HOLMES, AMANDA HOLMES, KALEIDOSCOPE MEDIA GROUP INC, ULTIMATE BUSINESS SUCCESS LLC, VICKEY HOLMES AND AMANDA HOLMES AS CO-TRUSTEES OF BUSINESS BREAKTHROUGHS INTERNATIONAL'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas To Snell Wilmer And Joshua Schneiderman (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash subpoena seeking deposition testimony of defendant/cross-complainant's transaction law firm/counsel and related production of the documents as limited during the "meet and confer" process to three categories identified in the opposition to the motion is denied. The Court finds the criteria and standards for deposition questioning of counsel in Spectra-Physics, Inc. vs. Superior Court (1988) 198 Cal. App. 3d 1487 have been met. The conditions for deposition questioning/ production of documents to proceed are as follows: (1) a Discovery Referee shall preside over the depositions and rule on all privilege and other objections. The Discovery Referee shall be chosen by agreement of the parties. If the parties cannot agree the Court shall choose the Discovery Referee based on nominations for same submitted on or before June 18, 2015. Any Discovery Referee nominated shall be a retired Judge of the Superior Court. All fees and costs of the Discovery Referee shall be borne by the plaintiff/cross-defendant and are recoverable as a cost in the action. (continued on part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARC PEARL VS. VICKEY HOLMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Pearls Notice Of Motion And Motion #1 Compelling Defendant Cross-Complainant Vickey Holmes Verification And Responses To Plaintiffs Document Request, Set One, And For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARC PEARL'S Motion #1 Compelling Defendant and Cross-Complainant Vickey Holmes Verification And Responses To Plaintiff's Document Request, Set One, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court reiterates its decision to order "measured discovery" in the context of the twenty four motions filed by the plaintiffs/cross-defendants and recent related discovery requests which are the subject of a motion to quash. See: Williams v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County, Court of Appeal, 2nd Appellate Dist., 1st Div. filed May 15, 2015. The parties shall bring respective proposed Orders relating to the scope, covered dates, and timing of e-Discovery as previously directed at the May 26, 2015 hearing. The parties shall be prepared to address plaintiffs' request for an evidentiary hearing on the subject of plaintiffs/cross-defendants establishment of a granting of equity in the defendant/cross-complainants organization entitling them to expanded damages discovery at this point in time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT WILLIAM SMITH VS. VICKEY HOLMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Smith'S Notice Of Motion And Motion #1 Compelling Defendant Vickey Holmes' Verification Responses To Plaintiff'S Document Request, Set One, And For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ROBERT SMITH'S Motion #1 Compelling Defendant Vickey Holmes' Verification and Responses To Plaintiff's Document Request, Set One, And For Sanctions. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Court reiterates its decision to order "measured discovery" in the context of the twenty four motions filed by the plaintiffs/cross-defendants and recent related discovery requests which are the subject of a motion to quash. See: Williams v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County, Court of Appeal, 2nd Appellate Dist., 1st Div. filed May 15, 2015. The parties shall bring respective proposed Orders relating to the scope, covered dates, and timing of e-Discovery as previously directed at the May 26, 2015 hearing. The parties shall be prepared to address plaintiffs' request for an evidentiary hearing on the subject of plaintiffs/cross-defendants establishment of a granting of equity in the defendant/cross-complainants organization entitling them to expanded damages discovery at this point in time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JORDANNE HO-SHUE et al VS. GDL SFO, INC. DBA GOLD DUST LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JORDANNE HO-SHUE's Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint The motion is granted. Defendant fails to show that the proposed amendments fail as a matter of law and the better practice in this circumstance, where the proposed claims involve factual issues, is to allow defendants to test the validity of the claims in a later proceeding. See Cal. Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1983) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280-281 (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390, 407). There is no prejudice in granting amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: UBIKA SOLUTIONS, INC VS. ZINIO, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Omnibus Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 9, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC'S Second Omnibus Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant/cross-complainant's unopposed motion is granted in part and denied in part. Granted is the motion to compel responses to written discovery described in its Notice of Motion within twenty days of service of this Order with Notice of Entry. Also granted is deeming defendant's request for admissions (second set) to be admitted. Sanctions are awarded in the sum of $1,556.00. Denied is the request for any future relief in the nature of evidentiary or terminating sanctions conditioned on compliance with the Order entered herein. A new and separate motion for that type of relief is required. Defendant shall prepare a proposed Order which specifically references the precise discovery to which written responses are due. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANNLIA PAGANINI HILL VS. THERESA FLANDRICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Theresa Flandrich'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2015, Line 14, Defendant Theresa Flandrich'S Demurrer To Complaint The demurrer to the complaint is overruled. Defendants have 5 days to answer. Defendant raises a factual issue that is outside the four corners of the complaint. The complaint and judicially noticeable documents do no clearly disclose what, if anything, plaintiff served on defendant "[w]ithin 15 days of delivery of a Subsection (f)(1) notice of intent to the Rent Board¿" Paragraph 13 of the complaint alleges the ultimate fact that defendant complied with all of the mandates of state and local law and the court must take this allegation as true. The court rejects plaintiff's law of the case/reconsideration argument and denies her request for sanctions. The Court of Appeal did not address the precise issue raised by the instant demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg
